_meta.note: Single source of truth for editable site content. Edit via admin.html, publish by replacing this file on the host.

siteInfo.heroSub: Ashforge Studio is a solo TTRPG content studio. I write adventures, lore, stat blocks, and supplements for publishers, creators, and Dungeon Masters who demand more from their worlds.
siteInfo.footerTagline: Professional D&D 5e writing and supplements by Matthias Moore.
siteInfo.oglNote: This work includes material taken from the System Reference Document 5.1 ("SRD 5.1") by Wizards of the Coast LLC and available at https://dnd.wizards.com/resources/systems-reference-document. The SRD 5.1 is licensed under the Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International License.

about.bio: I'm a solo TTRPG writer and the founder of Ashforge Studio LLC. I write adventures, lore, stat blocks, and supplements for 5th Edition Dungeons & Dragons - for publishers, creators, and Dungeon Masters who want more from their worlds.
about.pillar1Text: Every setting detail earns its place through play.
about.pillar2Text: Run cold. The setting reveals itself at the table.
about.pillar3Text: Compatible with the 5th Edition System Reference Document.
about.pillar4Text: Every adventure is playtested before release.

services.name: Adventure Module Writing
services.body: Full adventure modules from 1-shot encounters to multi-session campaigns. We handle premise, structure, room descriptions, stat block selection, and boxed text.
services.priceNote: Contact for quote - typically scoped by word count and complexity.

services.name: World Lore & Setting Writing
services.body: Pantheons, history, political factions, cultures, and regional lore. We build worlds that feel lived-in and give GMs the context they need to improvise with confidence.
services.priceNote: Contact for quote - flat-fee or per-document depending on scope.

services.name: Mechanical Design & Stat Blocks
services.body: Monster stat blocks, custom items, feats, spells, and subclasses. Tuned for 5e balance, playtested internally, and delivered in both prose and formatted layout.
services.priceNote: Per block / per item pricing - bundle rates for packs of 5+.

services.name: Article & Blog Writing
services.body: Long-form articles, blog posts, and editorial features on TTRPG craft, design theory, and play. SEO-aware, voice-matched to your publication, and delivered ready-to-publish.
services.priceNote: Per-article or per-word - recurring slate discounts available.

services.name: TTRPG Mechanic Review
services.body: An independent pass on your homebrew subclasses, spells, items, or monsters. I check balance against 5e baselines, flag exploit cases, and suggest tuning notes before you publish or playtest.
services.priceNote: Flat-fee per item or per packet - quote within 24 hours.

services.name: TTRPG Editing & Proofreading
services.body: Rules-aware editing from writers who know 5e inside and out. We check for mechanical inconsistencies, tone, clarity, pacing, and formatting - not just grammar.
services.priceNote: Per-word pricing - line edit and proof passes priced separately.

testimonials.quote: Would absolutely love to have back on our next project! The teammate every creator needs. Thoughtful, deliberate, and invested. A First Draft Pick.
testimonials.author: Kazmo Studios
testimonials.role: Outside the Wire · Core Mechanics, QA & Game Flow Editor

faq.a: Short pieces (stat blocks, single encounters, articles) usually land within 1–2 weeks. Full adventure modules and setting documents are scoped per-project, but most fall in the 3–6 week range. Rush work is possible when the calendar allows.

You do. Standard commissions are work-for-hire - full rights transfer to you on final payment, and I happily sign NDAs for unannounced projects. Bylined or co-credited arrangements are also fine; we'll just write it into the agreement.

Every project includes two rounds of revisions against the original brief. Larger structural rewrites past that are billed hourly. I send drafts in stages so you can steer early - most projects only need one revision pass because of it.

Most work is quoted per-project after a short brief - adventures and lore docs by scope, stat blocks per-item with bundle rates, editing per-word. I'll send a written quote within 24 hours of receiving your brief, and nothing starts until you've approved it.

faq.q: Is there a minimum project size?
faq.a: No formal minimum, but very small jobs (a single stat block, a one-paragraph rewrite) carry a small admin fee to make the back-and-forth worthwhile. Bundles of 3+ items waive it.

faq.q: Do you write for systems other than 5e?
faq.a: 5th Edition is the primary focus and where I'm sharpest on balance. I'm comfortable writing system-agnostic lore and narrative material for any TTRPG. For other rulesets (PF2e, OSR, etc.) I'll flag where I can deliver confidently versus where you'd be better off with a specialist.

Yes - both. Ghostwriting under your byline is a standard arrangement; co-writing where credit is shared works too. The only thing I won't do is write content I haven't been credited for that's then attributed to someone other than the buyer.

supplements.title: The Long Watch
supplements.description: A lone watchtower at the forest's edge, a light still burning in its window, and the ravens that never leave. The next dark-fantasy chapter of the Edenfall Collection - coming soon to DriveThruRPG.

credits.role: Core Mechanics Editor · Quality Assurance · Game Flow
credits.description: Tactical sandbox TTRPG by Derek Roberson, retired U.S. Marine. Modern military operations, solo or group play, decision-driven campaigns.
